title: Permission for Managing Tags (Line of Business and Topics)
story: As a product administrator, I want to control which users can add and manage categories (Line of Business and Topics), so that only authorized users can modify the tagging taxonomy.
description:
Add a general permission to control access to category/tag management. Users without this permission will see the 'Add' button suppressed and will not have access to the tag management spreadsheet on the Settings page.

The permission is a single general toggle — not per-tag — controlling whether a user can add, edit, or remove tags across all tag categories.
acceptance_criteria: New permission set for 'Manage Tags' added to the permissions system
Users without permission cannot see the 'Add' button for tags
Settings page tag spreadsheet is suppressed for users without permission
Permission applies generally across all tag types (not per individual tag)
ux_changes: 'Add' button suppressed for users without tag management permission. Tag spreadsheet hidden on Settings page.
middleware_changes: Permission check added to tag management actions and settings page rendering.
data_model_changes: New 'Manage Tags' permission added to the permissions/roles system.
implementation_notes: Tags (Line of Business, Topics) are dynamic and loaded from the database per customer. The permission is general rather than per-tag, simplifying management.
comment_summary: Developer asked whether permission should be per-tag or general. Confirmed general. New permission set added and implemented on development.
